text: "Holy of holies."... That is, most holy; as being dedicated to God, and set aside by his ordinance for the use of his priests.

"Without leaven or honey."... No leaven or honey was to be used in the sacrifice offered to God: to signify that we are to exclude from the pure worship of the gospel, all double-dealing and affection to carnal pleasures.
"Salt"... In every sacrifice salt was to be used, which is an emblem of wisdom and discretion, without which none of our performances are agreeable to God.
